headline: Teen Creates Hurricane Safety Program for Schools
meta_description: A Florida teenager developed Storm Smart, a hurricane preparedness curriculum, to help students learn vital safety skills their schools weren't teaching.
summary_short: After Hurricane Irma's impact on her community, a 16-year-old created an educational program on hurricane preparedness for fellow students.
body:
In South Florida, where hurricanes are a recurring threat, a 16-year-old recognized a gap in school education and decided to fill it herself. Elise Raurell developed Storm Smart as part of her Girl Scout Gold Award project, creating a practical curriculum on hurricane preparedness designed to fit into existing school lessons.

Having experienced the direct impact of Hurricane Irma on her community, Raurell understood firsthand why students need to understand how to prepare for severe weather. Rather than waiting for official institutions to act, she took initiative and built educational materials that could reach her peers.

The program represents the kind of grassroots problem-solving that strengthens communities. By equipping young people with knowledge about hurricane safety, Raurell has given them tools that could protect both themselves and their families when dangerous storms arrive.
